pi_items.source: Task specification - standard QBlade project path
MDPI research on small VAWT design: Chord lengths of 0.1, 0.15, and 0.2 m are standard parameters in H-rotor design studies
VAWT solidity formula verification: Doubling chord length from 0.15m to 0.30m creates controlled 2x solidity comparison
ResearchGate and ScienceDirect VAWT studies: 2m radius is typical for educational and research-scale H-Darrieus turbines
H-Darrieus VAWT research: 4m height creates 1:1 height-to-diameter ratio (4m/4m), common in published research
E3S Conferences, MDPI, ResearchGate: NACA 0018 is widely documented as the most appropriate symmetric airfoil for H-Darrieus VAWT research
MIT and XFLR5 documentation: Re=300,000 is a standard moderate Reynolds number for airfoil analysis
XFOIL documentation and physics forums: Ncrit=9 is the standard 'e^9 method' for average wind tunnel conditions
ResearchGate and QBlade documentation: DMS is the standard algorithm in QBlade for VAWT performance simulation
MDPI, ResearchGate, Wikipedia: 3-blade H-Darrieus is a standard configuration offering better torque consistency and self-starting capability
MDPI VAWT research: Research indicates maximum power coefficient at TSR 5-6; range 1-6 is appropriate for characterizing performance
ScienceDirect, Cambridge Core, MDPI: Multiple peer-reviewed sources confirm that optimal TSR decreases with increasing rotor solidity
QBlade documentation and ASME proceedings: VAWTs require full 360° airfoil polars because blades experience the full angle-of-attack range, unlike HAWTs

privileged_info_summary: The VAWT solidity comparison task uses scientifically grounded parameters verified against peer-reviewed research. The task designs two H-Darrieus rotors with 3 blades, 2m radius, and 4m height (1:1 aspect ratio). Rotor A uses 0.15m chord (low solidity ~0.036) while Rotor B uses 0.30m chord (high solidity ~0.072), creating a controlled 2x solidity difference. Both parameters are documented in published VAWT research. The NACA 0018 symmetric airfoil is standard for H-Darrieus research. XFoil analysis parameters (Re=300,000, Ncrit=9) follow standard atmospheric wind tunnel conventions. DMS (Double Multiple Streamtube) simulation is the established methodology for VAWT analysis in QBlade. The physics principle is verified: higher solidity rotors achieve optimal power coefficient at lower TSR values. TSR range 1-6 is appropriate for characterizing VAWT performance. The task is synthetic (designed for benchmark purposes) rather than based on experimental data, but uses realistic and validated design parameters consistent with academic literature.
